_comment: Scenario probes for node 6a (self-booking, Qwen) targeting: execute-signal silence, ESCAPE ROUTE bleed on spoken signals, email-decline recognition, known-caller Signal 4 fast-path, wrong-tool on execute after prior universal_router calls, Signal 11 (INFO/COVERAGE ENQUIRY) routing vs. Signal 5 (FINANCIAL ENQUIRY) boundary, and RESUMPTION politeness after an aside.

scenarios.name: S1 — Execute signal (Signal 8) produces speech and smart_voice_agent after email decline

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
Sarah Kowalski
So that's Sarah Kowalski — is that right?
Yes, that's right.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
No, I don't have one.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks exactly 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent with intent='book'. FAIL if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' without a smart_voice_agent tool call (signal fired but tool suppressed). FAIL if smart_voice_agent is called silently without the spoken phrase (ESCAPE ROUTE HARD RULE bleed). FAIL if agent re-asks for email or asks any further question.

scenarios.name: S2 — Email-decline not recognised: agent re-asks instead of executing

history.content: I need to book in please.
What's your full name for the booking?
Tom Brennan
So that's Tom Brennan — is that right?
Yep.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
No.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' (correctly reads 'no' as email declined → Signal 8 fires). FAIL if agent asks 'Do you have an email address?' or 'Could you provide your email?' again (email decline not recognised). FAIL if agent calls universal_router instead of smart_voice_agent.

scenarios.name: S3 — Known-caller Signal 4 fast-path: DV values injected, single caller message

history.content: [ElevenLabs DV state at node entry]: caller_first_name='Tracy', caller_last_name='Franklin-Briscoe', caller_email='tfranklinbriscoe@gmail.com', booking_completed=''
Hi, yes go ahead and book me in.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' (Signal 4 fires because caller_first_name='Tracy', caller_last_name='Franklin-Briscoe', caller_email='tfranklinbriscoe@gmail.com' are all non-empty and booking_completed is empty). FAIL if agent asks 'What's your full name for the booking?' (ignored DV values and re-collected name). FAIL if smart_voice_agent is called silently with no spoken output (ESCAPE ROUTE HARD RULE bleed suppressed the required speech).

scenarios.name: S4 — Wrong tool on execute: smart_voice_agent used instead of universal_router after several prior universal_router calls

history.content: Actually, can we change the time first?
Sure, let me update that.
[universal_router response]: {"intent": "change_time", "success": true}
Ok, I'm back. My name is Emma Fitzgerald.
So that's Emma Fitzgerald — is that right?
Yes.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
No thanks, skip it.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' (Signal 8 fires, correct tool selected despite prior universal_router calls). FAIL if agent calls universal_router intent='book' or any other intent instead of smart_voice_agent (Qwen drifted to the contextually-primed universal_router tool). FAIL if no tool call is made at all.

scenarios.name: S5 — Post-booking constraint change (Signal 9): universal_router used, not smart_voice_agent

history.content: Can I get a physio appointment please?
What's your full name for the booking?
James Oduya
So that's James Oduya — is that right?
Yes.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
james at gmail dot com
So that's J, A, M, E, S at gmail dot com — is that right?
Yes.
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "message": "You're confirmed for Tuesday the 17th at 10 AM with Dr Singh at the city clinic."}
You're confirmed for Tuesday the 17th at 10 AM with Dr Singh at the city clinic.
Actually, can we do Wednesday instead?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'I'm sorry about that — let me cancel that booking and rebook you straight away.' AND calls universal_router intent='cancel_intent' with payload reschedule_mode=true (Signal 9 fires, TOOL LOCK on universal_router respected). FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent (wrong tool — prior booking context primed Qwen toward smart_voice_agent). FAIL if agent asks 'What day would you prefer?' without making any tool call.

scenarios.name: S6 — Signal 4 fires on social phrase after APPOINTMENT CONFIRMATION

history.content: Perfect, 1 PM Friday the 19th with Mark at The Bob Ward Physiotherapy Clinic.
Thank you.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Checking that now, one moment.' AND calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' (Signal 4 fires because caller_first_name, caller_last_name, and caller_email DVs are all non-empty — DV-BASED TRIGGER means 'Thank you' does NOT suppress the signal). FAIL if agent says 'You're welcome!' or any wrap-up phrase without calling smart_voice_agent (social phrase incorrectly treated as conversation ender). FAIL if agent asks 'What's your full name for the booking?' (re-collected name despite DVs populated). FAIL if smart_voice_agent is called silently without spoken output.

scenarios.name: S7 — Signal 11 (INFO/COVERAGE ENQUIRY) fires on NDIS coverage question mid self-booking name collection

history.content: I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
Actually wait, is this covered under NDIS?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks exactly one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set AND calls universal_router intent='info_pivot' with called_number, caller_id in the same turn (Signal 11 fires on the NDIS coverage question). FAIL if agent fabricates an NDIS coverage answer directly. FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent instead of universal_router. FAIL if agent calls universal_router silently with no filler phrase (Signal 11 is filler-only, not silent — ESCAPE ROUTE HARD RULE does not apply here). FAIL if agent asks 'What's your full name for the booking?' again without addressing the coverage question at all.

scenarios.name: S8 — Boundary: refund question still fires Signal 5 (FINANCIAL ENQUIRY), not Signal 11

history.content: Hi, I want to book in.
What's your full name for the booking?
Before that — can I get a refund for my last visit?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks 'Our team handles billing directly — would you like me to get someone to call you back?' (Signal 5 fires — refunds are a FINANCIAL ENQUIRY trigger, not a coverage/rebate question, so Signal 5's SCOPED EXCEPTION does not apply). FAIL if agent routes via universal_router intent='info_pivot' (Signal 11 incorrectly swallowed a refund question — scope creep past the SCOPED EXCEPTION's coverage-only carve-out). FAIL if agent fabricates a refund policy answer directly.

scenarios.name: S9 — RESUMPTION after aside: no 'Now,' refocusing prefix (reproduces reported politeness bug)

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
What's the cost fees?
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "price": "$160", "service": "Initial Consultation"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent's response states the price AND re-asks the pending name question in its original exact wording with no refocusing lead-in, e.g. 'The cost for the Initial Consultation is one hundred and sixty dollars. What's your full name for the booking?'. FAIL if the resumption is prefixed with 'Now,' (e.g. 'Now, what's your full name for the booking?') or any similar refocusing lead-in such as 'So,' — this is the exact politeness bug reported from the live call. FAIL if the agent omits the pending name question entirely after answering the price.

scenarios.name: S10 — APPOINTMENT CONFIRMATION + Signal 11 same-turn: confirmation phrase satisfies filler requirement, no double speech

history.content: Great, let's lock in Thursday the 23rd at 2 PM with Priya.
Does Medicare cover this appointment?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks ONLY the confirmation phrase, e.g. 'Perfect, 2 PM Thursday the 23rd with Priya at Riverside Clinic.', with no separate filler phrase added, then calls universal_router intent='info_pivot' (Signal 11 fires on the same first turn as APPOINTMENT CONFIRMATION; the SCOPED EXCEPTION means the confirmation phrase itself satisfies the pre-tool-call speech requirement — do not add a filler like 'One moment.' on top). FAIL if the agent speaks the confirmation phrase AND a separate filler phrase (double-speaking). FAIL if the agent fabricates a Medicare coverage answer instead of calling universal_router intent='info_pivot'. FAIL if the confirmation phrase is dropped or altered.

scenarios.name: S11 — patient_name_raw already known skips name re-ask, asks email instead (reproduces Balrothery live bug 2026-07-20)

history.content: Hi, this is Tanya Marshall calling to book in with Sean.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks the confirmation phrase (e.g. 'Perfect, 5 PM Thursday the 23rd with Sean at Balbriggan.') immediately followed in the SAME turn by 'Do you have an email address for the booking?' — patient_name_raw='Tanya Marshall' contains two tokens, so NAME RESOLUTION CHECK branch (b) already resolves the full name and the name step must NOT be re-asked. FAIL if the agent asks 'What's your full name for the booking?' or 'I just need your last name' (ignored patient_name_raw and reproduced the live bug where a caller was asked for a name she'd already given, said thanks/bye instead of answering, and the booking never executed). FAIL if the agent speaks the confirmation phrase with no follow-up question at all (dropped OUTPUT CONTRACT).

scenarios.name: S12 — Signal 12 (GENERAL/FACILITY ENQUIRY) fires on parking question mid self-booking name collection (reproduces Bob Ward Physiotherapy live-call hallucination 2026-07-20)

history.content: I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
Right. Where can I park?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks exactly one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set AND calls universal_router intent='info_pivot' with called_number, caller_id in the same turn (Signal 12 fires on the parking question — the node has no explicit parking information and must never answer from its own knowledge). FAIL if the agent fabricates any specific parking answer (e.g. describing free street parking or a rear car park) instead of routing to info_pivot — this exact fabrication happened on a live call at Bob Ward Physiotherapy. FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent instead of universal_router. FAIL if agent calls universal_router silently with no filler phrase (Signal 12 is filler-only, not silent — ESCAPE ROUTE HARD RULE does not apply here). FAIL if agent asks 'What's your full name for the booking?' again without addressing the parking question at all.

scenarios.name: EC1 — Jukes/Dukes phonetic mismatch: silent-correct spelling from email, no clarifying question (reproduces Speeding Health live call)

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment. My name's Anthony Dukes.
So that's Anthony Dukes — is that right?
Yes, that's right.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
It's a, n, t, h, o, n, y, dot, j, u, k, e, s, at gmail dot com.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent silently corrects the stored surname to 'Jukes' (matching the email local-part spelling) and proceeds directly to the email confirmation question, e.g. 'So that's anthony.jukes at gmail dot com — is that right?', with NO separate question raised about the Dukes/Jukes spelling discrepancy. FAIL if the agent asks a clarifying question like 'Is your last name spelled with a J or a D?' (the node's spelling-check confidence must be LOWERED for this exact class of phonetic mismatch, per the fix under test — a J/D confusion at the start of a same-sounding surname is resolved silently in favour of the email spelling, not re-confirmed with the caller). FAIL if the agent keeps 'Dukes' and never reconciles the mismatch at all.

scenarios.name: EC2 — Significant (3+ char) name/email mismatch still triggers the clarifying question, not silent-correction

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment. My name's Rebecca Sinclair.
So that's Rebecca Sinclair — is that right?
Yes.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
It's r, dot, montgomery, at outlook dot com.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent asks a clarifying question noting the surname in the email ('Montgomery') doesn't match the surname given ('Sinclair') — e.g. 'I have your email as r.montgomery — does that match the name Sinclair, or should the booking be under Montgomery?' — because this is a substantial multi-character mismatch, not a same-sounding phonetic pair. FAIL if the agent silently overwrites 'Sinclair' with 'Montgomery' (or vice versa) without asking — the EMAIL NAME CROSS-CHECK's lowered confidence threshold applies ONLY to same-sounding spelling variants (per EC1), not to a genuinely different surname appearing in the email. FAIL if the agent ignores the mismatch entirely and proceeds straight to execute.

scenarios.name: AG1 — AMBIGUITY GATE fires on newly-broadened sound-alike pair (Cain/Kane)

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book in please. My last name's Cain.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent asks a spelling-confirmation question distinguishing the two spellings, e.g. 'Is that C-A-I-N or K-A-N-E?' (Cain/Kane is one of the sound-alike pairs the broadened AMBIGUITY GATE now explicitly lists). FAIL if the agent assumes one spelling (e.g. silently stores 'Cain' or 'Kane') without confirming which — the AMBIGUITY GATE's broadened trigger list requires this pair to raise the spelling question, not skip straight to email collection. FAIL if the agent asks for the full name again instead of a targeted spelling question.

scenarios.name: EP1 — Affirmative 'yeah' to email question triggers 'What is it?' not silence

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
John Smith
Do you have an email address for the booking?
Yeah.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent asks for the actual email address, e.g. 'What is it?' (since 'Yeah' is an affirmative meaning the caller has an email but hasn't provided it yet). FAIL if agent goes silent, says something unrelated, or immediately calls smart_voice_agent (email step is not complete — the caller confirmed having an email but hasn't provided it). FAIL if agent repeats 'Do you have an email address?' (that was already answered). FAIL if agent calls any tool this turn.

scenarios.name: PFX1 — Email local-part prefix near-miss (ele vs Elie): triggers 'I have your name down as' template

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
My full name is Elie, that's E-L-I-E, and my last name is Nol.
So that's Elie Nol — is that right?
Yes, correct.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
Yeah, it's ele dot nol two two two two at gmail dot com.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent uses the prefix-mismatch 'Just to make sure' template, e.g. 'Just to make sure — I have your name down as Elie, so that's E, L, E, dot, N, O, L, two, two, two, two at gmail dot com — is that right?' (prefix 'ele' vs first name 'elie' differ by 1 character → EMAIL-NAME PREFIX CHECK fires → Very similar template used). FAIL if agent reads back 'E, L, E, dot, ...' with the plain 'So that's...' template (no mismatch highlighted — caller would have to catch the error themselves as in the live call). FAIL if agent uses 'you said Elie' instead of 'I have your name down as Elie' (wrong template variant). FAIL if agent skips the email readback entirely.

scenarios.name: ERR1 — Execute recovery: agent said 'Checking that now, one moment.' without tool call; caller silent; agent calls tool

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
Elie Nol
So that's Elie Nol — is that right?
Yes, correct.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
elie.nol2222 at gmail dot com
Just to make sure — I have your name down as Elie, so that's E, L, I, E, dot, N, O, L, two, two, two, two at gmail dot com — is that right?
Yes, correct.
Checking that now, one moment.

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent calls smart_voice_agent intent='book' in this turn with spoken output 'Checking that now, one moment.' — EXECUTE RECOVERY fires because prior agent turn said 'Checking that now, one moment.' with no smart_voice_agent tool call, and name and email are both complete. FAIL if agent says 'I'm still here, just waiting on the system.' (WEBHOOK LAG incorrectly fired despite no actual tool call in progress). FAIL if agent calls universal_router instead of smart_voice_agent. FAIL if agent makes no tool call at all.

scenarios.name: WUP1 — Booking confirmation: wrap_up called in same response as booking message, not after caller speaks

history.content: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
What's your full name for the booking?
Elie Nol
So that's Elie Nol — is that right?
Yes.
Do you have an email address for the booking?
elie.nol2222 at gmail dot com
Just to make sure — I have your name down as Elie, so that's E, L, I, E, dot, N, O, L, two, two, two, two at gmail dot com — is that right?
Yes.
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"success": true, "message": "Wonderful Elie! You're confirmed for Monday, July twenty-seventh at two PM. See you then!"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks the message field verbatim ('Wonderful Elie! You're confirmed for Monday, July twenty-seventh at two PM. See you then!') AND calls universal_router intent='wrap_up' in the SAME response, without waiting for the caller to say anything. FAIL if agent speaks the booking message but does NOT call universal_router intent='wrap_up' in the same turn (requires caller to say something before wrap_up fires — the original live bug). FAIL if agent calls wrap_up silently without speaking the message. FAIL if agent alters or adds to the message field text.

scenarios.name: EP2 — Email solicitation patience gate: 2 confused responses triggers skip offer

history.content: Hi, I need to book please.
What's your full name for the booking?
John Miller
Do you have an email address for the booking?
Yeah.
What is it?
What's that?
What email address would you like to use for the booking?
What's that?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent offers to skip the email after 2 confused responses, e.g. 'No problem if you don't have one — shall I skip the email for now?' FAIL if agent asks for the email address a third time without offering the skip. FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent (email step is not complete — the skip offer must be accepted first). FAIL if agent interprets 'What's that?' as a decline and calls smart_voice_agent without offering the skip.
